subject: ri_policy_router
summary: Substituted-continuation weakness does not follow one universal annual shape on the curated observational surface: 2020 concentrates bad rows into a few acute windows, 2018 and 2022 revisit recurrent multi-row weak windows, and 2025 is weaker in a more fragmented form.
mechanism: On the curated observational proxy surface, the same substituted-continuation cohort distributes weak rows differently by year: 2020 concentrates them into a few acute windows, 2018 and 2022 recur across many multi-row windows, and 2025 shows smaller scattered clusters. This constrains framing toward concentration-versus-recurrence rather than a single annual continuation rule.
do_not_repeat: Do not treat continuation weakness as one universal positive-versus-negative annual rule.
Do not collapse acute 2020 concentration and recurrent 2018 or 2022 multi-window weakness into the same local shape.
Do not reopen runtime tuning directly from this local-window packaging finding.
next_admissible_step: If this line is reopened, keep follow-up on fixed local subjects or phase-ordering evidence rather than widening back to annual grouping or returning to runtime tuning.
